description: Seed patterns for the audit/smart-contracts family (Solidity / EVM). Hits target state-changing entry points, access-control modifiers, low-level call surfaces, value-handling, upgradeability, oracle / external data dependencies, and post-Cancun footguns. The downstream skill (ToB building-secure-contracts, dispatched after entry-point-analyzer maps the contract's actions) verifies reentrancy guards, msg.sender vs tx.origin, unchecked external calls, and integer / unit pitfalls.
notes: Solidity has no separate manifest layer worth scanning; all signal lives in the *.sol files themselves. Foundry / Hardhat configs are out of scope for this family — the supply-chain family covers package.json / foundry.toml install hooks.
Patterns are deliberately broad anchors: `function .* external`, `selfdestruct`, `delegatecall`. The skill is expected to read the surrounding function to judge whether each anchor is dangerous.

`entry-point-analyzer` runs as a dependency before this skill, so the per-packet skill already has a contract-level map of state-changing actions. The patterns here are what feeds that analyzer in the first place.

solidity.description: Externally callable function — primary entry point surface
solidity.false_positive_notes: Stable; every external function is a candidate for access-control / reentrancy review.

solidity.description: Public function — callable externally and internally
Payable function / receive / fallback — accepts ETH
solidity.false_positive_notes: Verify reentrancy guard and pull-payment pattern.
Read the modifier body — silent reverts / missing _ are common bugs.
Presence is positive; the question is which entry points lack one.

solidity.description: Inline msg.sender == X check (ad-hoc access control)
solidity.false_positive_notes: Common; verify the compared value is constant / immutable / role-mapped.
Almost always a finding — tx.origin enables phishing-via-malicious-contract.

solidity.description: delegatecall — runs target code in *this* contract's storage
solidity.false_positive_notes: Storage-layout / function-selector clash; if target is upgradeable, audit migration.
Post-Cancun behaviour changed; still a high-impact authority surface.
Gas-stipend assumption breaks for some recipients; prefer .call with checks.
Bypasses Solidity's safety checks; manual review required.
Verify that no attacker-controlled value can over/underflow inside.
Check for signature malleability, nonce reuse, missing chain-id / domain separator.
Audit storage-layout, init-vs-constructor, and admin-key risk.
